dialogue.text: You've been real quiet tonight, hon. Everything okay on patrol?
Yeah, just... some guy tried to rob a bank with a crowbar. Felt kinda pointless stopping him, y'know?
It's not pointless. You saved someone from getting hurt. That's what matters.
I know, Mom. Just... some days it feels like I'm barely making a dent, you know? But thanks.

meta.characters: Mark (the teenage superhero, Invincible) and Debbie (his mom)
meta.context: Mark has been avoiding talking about his patrol today. Debbie notices he's quiet.

dialogue.text: Hey, Mom. I was just... looking at this old photo.
That was from before we moved into this house. You were what, nine?
Yeah. I just don't get how he could pretend for so long. Like, was any of it real?
I've asked myself that every day since he left. But Mark, the way he looked at you in that picture? That was real.
I guess... I'm just tired of being the son of Omni-Man. I want to be my own kind of hero.
You already are, sweetheart. You always have been.
Thanks, Mom. I think I needed to hear that.
Anytime. Now come help me with dinner. I'm making your favorite.

meta.characters: Mark (struggling with his father's legacy), Debbie (his mom, trying to comfort him)
meta.context: After Omni-Man betrayed Earth and left, Mark is dealing with complicated feelings about his dad.

dialogue.text: Hey, Mom. You're still up? That's... probably not a good sign.
Mark. Your face. What happened this time?
It's nothing, okay? Just... some guy with a grudge. I handled it.
You don't have to pretend with me. I'm your mom. Just... tell me the truth.

meta.characters: Mark (trying to act tough), Debbie (worried mom)
meta.context: Mark had a rough fight but doesn't want to worry his mom.

dialogue.text: Look, I stopped the robbers, okay? What's the big deal?
The big deal is you froze for three seconds while a civilian was in the line of fire. Three seconds is a lifetime in this job.
I didn't freeze—I was just... thinking. Making sure I didn't punch through a wall and hit someone on the other side.
Thinking gets people killed, Mark. You have to trust your instincts. Hesitation is a luxury you can't afford.
Yeah... I guess you're right. I just don't want to make things worse. But I can't keep second-guessing myself either.
Good. That's the first step. Now, what are you going to do about it?
I'm gonna train harder. Run drills until the right move is automatic. No more hesitation.
That's what I like to hear. Report to the training facility tomorrow at 0600. Don't be late.

meta.characters: Mark (Invincible, still learning), Cecil (stern mentor figure)
meta.context: Mark hesitated during the fight and a civilian almost got hurt. Cecil is critical.

dialogue.text: I'm trying, Dad. I just—I can't get the rotation right. It's like my body won't listen.
Trying isn't enough. Your mother's planet is counting on you, and you can't even execute a basic aerial recovery.
You think I don't know that? Every time I screw up, I hear your voice in my head telling me I'm a failure!
Good. Then let that anger teach you. Stop thinking. Feel the air. Now do it again—and this time, don't miss.

meta.context: Omni-Man expects more from Mark. Mark is trying but feels like he'll never be good enough.

dialogue.text: Hey — I'm gonna get you out of here, okay? Just stay with me.
I-I can't move my leg... the ceiling's gonna fall!
I got you. Look at me — I'm Invincible, right? I catch falling buildings for a living. This one's no big deal.
You... you promise?
Promise. Now, on three — one, two, three — we go.
Okay... okay, I trust you.
That's my girl. Hold tight — we're about to fly.
Thank you... thank you so much.

meta.characters: Mark (trying to stay confident and reassuring), a scared civilian girl
meta.context: Mark needs to calm her down and get her to safety while the building keeps shaking.

dialogue.text: Go away, Eve.
Not happening. You look like you got hit by a truck.
I tried to do it alone. Thought I could handle him. People got hurt because I'm an idiot.
You're not an idiot. You're someone who cares too much and forgot that even superheroes need backup sometimes.
Yeah... maybe. Thanks for not saying 'I told you so.'

meta.characters: Mark (devastated after losing a fight), Eve (fellow superhero, caring)
meta.context: Mark tried to stop a villain alone and got badly beaten. People got hurt because of him.

dialogue.text: Look, I know I'm new here, but diving in headfirst without a plan is just gonna get someone killed.
Yeah, because you've got so much experience. Stick to the sidelines, kid, and let the adults handle it.
I'm not saying I know everything. I'm saying your 'plan' is just 'hit them hard and hope for the best.' That's not a plan, that's a tantrum.
...Fine. You got a better idea? Let's hear it, Grayson. But if it sucks, I'm doing it my way.

meta.context: Rex thinks Mark is too inexperienced. Mark thinks Rex is reckless.

dialogue.text: Whoa, hey — you made it. Thought you got abducted by aliens or something.
Ha, yeah, no aliens. Just... overslept. You know how it is.
Dude, you've 'overslept' like four times this week. What's going on?
Look, I can't really explain right now. But I'm fine. I promise. Just... trust me?

meta.characters: Mark (out of breath, making excuses), William (his best friend, suspicious)
meta.context: Mark has been skipping class a lot lately because of hero work. William is starting to notice.

dialogue.text: Hey, sorry—I'm here. Just... can we go over the quadratic formula one more time?
Mark, your phone's been buzzing every five minutes. You okay?
Yeah, fine. It's just... you know. Work stuff. Nothing major.
It's not 'nothing' if it's making you check it mid-sentence. Want to take a break?
No, I don't wanna waste your time. You came all the way here to help me cram, and I can't even focus for ten minutes.
You're not wasting my time. I'm here for you, not just the flashcards. Talk to me.
I just... I keep thinking about the city, about my dad, about failing this stupid history exam. It's all hitting at once.
Okay, first: you're not your dad. Second: we'll figure out the history thing together. And third... you're allowed to be human, Mark.
Thanks, Amber. Really. Let me just turn this thing off for an hour.
Good. Now, let's get you that A. One equation at a time.

meta.context: Mark keeps getting calls on his hero phone. He's trying to balance school and being Invincible.

dialogue.text: Mark, you said you'd be at the library studying yesterday. I called. They said you never showed.
Oh, uh, yeah—I was there, just in a different section. You know how loud the main floor gets.
Don't lie to me. I checked the whole building. Where were you really?
I... I can't tell you. It's complicated, okay? Just trust me—it's not what you think.
Then tell me what it is. Because right now, all I see is you disappearing and lying, and I'm tired of it.
Amber, I swear, I'm not—look, there's stuff going on. Stuff I can't explain. I want to, I really do, but if I tell you... it could put you in danger.

meta.context: Amber has noticed Mark keeps disappearing and lying about where he goes. She's confronting him.

dialogue.text: Hey, honey. Can I sit?
...Yeah. Sure.
You haven't touched your dinner in three days. I'm not gonna push, but... I'm here.
I know, Mom. It's just... I keep thinking if I could've said something different. Made him stay.
Oh, baby... none of this is your fault. He made his choice.
But what if I wasn't enough? What if I'm just... not who he wanted me to be?
You are exactly who you're supposed to be. And I am so proud of you. So, so proud.
Thanks, Mom. I... I don't know what I'd do without you.
You'll never have to find out. I'm right here.
Yeah... I know. I love you.

meta.characters: Mark (numb, grieving), Debbie (heartbroken herself but trying to be strong)
meta.context: It's been a week since Omni-Man left. Mark hasn't talked about it with anyone.

dialogue.text: I almost got that kid killed, Eve. The guy was about to punch through my skull, and I just... froze.
You didn't freeze. You pulled him out of the way. That's not freezing, that's making a split-second call.
Yeah, after I let him get that close. My dad could've ended that fight in two seconds. I'm just... not him.
Good. You're not your dad. You're the guy who feels awful about almost messing up. That's what makes you better than him.

meta.context: Mark is questioning whether he's cut out to be a hero after a close call.

dialogue.text: She... she said I lied about everything. Like I was just some... some story she was in.
Yeah, I heard. That's rough, man. But you did kind of disappear a lot.
I know. I just thought... if I saved people enough, it would make up for it.
Maybe you can't save someone by keeping them in the dark. Even if you're saving the whole world.

meta.context: Amber broke up with Mark because of all the lies. He finally understands how much he hurt her.

dialogue.text: You're wrong. There's gotta be some mistake, right? He wouldn't... he couldn't have.
I've got the body scans, Mark. And the security footage. I wish I was wrong.
So what, he just... snapped? One day he's making me PB&Js, the next he's tearing people apart like they're nothing?
This wasn't a snap. It was a mission. He was never really here, Mark. I'm sorry.
Then what am I supposed to do? He's my dad. I'm half him. What if I'm just... waiting to break too?

meta.context: Mark just learned his dad killed the original Guardians of the Globe. He doesn't know how to process it.

dialogue.text: So, uh, does it come in any other colors? Not that I'm complaining—this is awesome.
Yellow and blue's the standard for new recruits. The fabric's a reactive polymer—bulletproof, fire-resistant, and it breathes.
Wait, bulletproof? So I could, like, take a tank round and just—*flexes arm*—bounce it off?
In theory. But you'll still feel it. Try not to test it today—I'd rather not fill out the damage report.
Yeah, yeah, I got it. No tank rounds before lunch. But seriously—this feels like a real superhero suit. Like I actually belong now.
You do belong, kid. Now hold still—the shoulder pads need a final calibration.

meta.context: Mark is getting his first official Invincible costume. He's trying to play it cool but clearly loves it.

dialogue.text: Dude, you inhaled that burger. Did you even chew?
What can I say? Being a hero works up an appetite. ...I mean, uh, being a hungry teenager. Same thing.
Right. Sure. So, any *other* reasons you're always starving? Like, say, punching bad guys?
William, I told you—it's just the school track team. Really intense cardio. Now pass the ketchup before I have to use my super-speed to get it myself.

meta.context: William is trying to get Mark to admit something about his secret life. Mark deflects with humor.

dialogue.text: Okay, this time for sure—whoa, whoa, WHOA—
Dude, you just plowed a trench through Mrs. Chen's petunias again.
I'm not built for grace, okay? I'm built for... face-to-ground impact.
Maybe try... not aiming at the ground this time?
You're a genius. Now watch me nail it—or at least crash in a new direction.
I'll bring a camera next time. This is gold.
Laugh all you want. I'm getting this right if it kills the petunias.

meta.context: Mark keeps crashing into the ground. He's frustrated but refuses to give up.

dialogue.text: You're actually letting me win at this, aren't you?
Nope. You're just that good tonight, champ.
I don't know, Mom... feels nice. Just this.
Yeah. It really does. I love you, Mark.

meta.characters: Mark (trying to be normal), Debbie (his mom, cherishing the quiet moment)
meta.context: After everything that happened, they're both grateful for a normal evening.

dialogue.text: Hey. I know it's late. I just... couldn't sleep.
It's okay. I wasn't really asleep either. What's going on?
I keep having this dream where I'm just... not fast enough. Not strong enough. And everyone I love is gone.
Mark... you're not your dad. You've never been him. You've already saved so many people.
Yeah, but what if one day I can't? What if I'm the reason someone else gets hurt?
Then you'll get back up. Like you always do. And I'll be right there with you, every step.
...Thanks. That actually helps. Sorry for calling so late.
Don't apologize. Just promise me you'll try to get some rest, okay? I'll still be here in the morning.
Okay. Goodnight, Eve.
Goodnight, Mark. I'll text you before patrol.

meta.characters: Mark (scared, vulnerable), Eve (on the phone, trying to reassure him)
meta.context: Mark has been having nightmares about not being strong enough to protect the people he loves.

dialogue.text: I don't know if I can keep doing this, Eve.
What do you mean? You're Invincible, Mark.
That's the thing. I'm not. I'm just some kid who keeps getting his face smashed in.
You saved a city today. That's not nothing.
Yeah, and my dad almost killed me. Again. What's the point if it just never stops hurting?
The point is you're not your dad. You stop because you want to, not because you have to.
...Maybe I just need a break. A real one.
Then take one. But don't quit because you're scared — that's not who you are.
When did you get so smart?
When I stopped pretending I could fix everything alone.

meta.context: After a particularly brutal week, Mark questions if being a hero is worth the cost.

dialogue.text: I can still hear him, Art. Every time I miss a punch, he's right there in my head telling me I'm not good enough.
That voice doesn't have to be yours, Mark. The real question is, whose voice are you gonna listen to?
I don't know anymore. It's like... part of me still wants his approval, even after everything he did.
You're not weak for wanting that. You're human. But being strong means knowing when to let go.
I just... I don't want to become him. I'm scared that if I'm not careful, I'll end up the same way.

meta.context: Mark can still hear his dad's voice telling him he's weak. Art helps him work through it.
